summary.intent: The user wanted to understand how deterministic compaction (just merged in PR #653) compares to LLM-based approaches used in other codebases, then implement a multi-mode compaction system with proper config, docs, and tracking issues.
summary.outcome: Filed a beads epic (moltis-dxw) with 4 child issues, implemented CompactionConfig/CompactionMode enum (Deterministic, RecencyPreserving, Structured, LlmReplace), restored the LlmReplace path behind a cargo feature flag, wrote docs, updated the config template, and committed all scaffolding to the feat/deterministic-compaction branch.

repo: moltis uses a worktree layout at ~/.superset/worktrees/moltis/<fork>/<branch>/ for PR work

Config schema lives in crates/config/src/schema.rs, with re-exports in lib.rs, validation in validate.rs, and user-facing template in template.rs — all four files must be updated together for a new config key
The chat crate's compact_session is the canonical compaction entry point; it was previously LLM-only, replaced by deterministic extraction in PR #653
cargo feature llm-compaction must be forwarded through the dependency chain: moltis-chat → moltis-gateway → moltis-cli to reach the final binary
moltis-csq (llama-cpp-sys) has a known CMake/CUDA build failure on macOS that is unrelated to application code — clippy on the full workspace will always surface this
just lint is the canonical lint recipe; it runs an OS-aware clippy invocation
beads is the project management tool; issues are referenced as moltis-<id> and epics link child issues with dependency wiring

code.finding: New CompactionConfig struct and CompactionMode enum added here; ChatConfig gets a compaction: CompactionConfig field
build_schema_map must be updated for every new config struct; semantic warnings for LLM modes without a summary_model go near the existing workspace_file_max_chars check
New dispatcher module routing CompactionMode variants; llm_replace path gated behind #[cfg(feature = 'llm-compaction')]
compact_session now takes &CompactionConfig and delegates to compaction_run::run; ChatService::compact does the same; call site in run_with_tools passes config ref
New doc page explaining all four compaction modes with trade-off table, config reference, and upgrade guidance
New [chat.compaction] section with rich inline comments describing each mode added to the generated config template

workflow: Cross-repo analysis (openclaw, hermes-agent) was essential for informing the design — reading reference implementations before proposing modes avoided reinventing solved problems
Greptile re-runs automatically on new commits and its check result (0 comments added) is a useful signal that review findings were addressed
Checking no-default-features builds separately from default builds catches cfg-gated unused import warnings that normal builds hide
Format issues surfaced iteratively across multiple fmt recheck cycles — running fmt check before committing is mandatory
hermes-agent's tool-output pruning pre-pass (phase 1, no LLM) is the highest bang-for-buck compaction improvement not yet in moltis

friction: Multiple fmt recheck cycles were needed (4+ rounds) because compaction_run.rs had repeated formatting violations — the file was written incrementally and each edit introduced new fmt issues
The no-default-features build revealed a conditional-import issue requiring additional cfg gates on error enum variants after the main build was already clean
The pre-PR LLM compaction code was deleted (~95 lines); it had to be reconstructed from git history rather than being available to read directly
The moltis-csq CMake/CUDA failure on macOS makes workspace-level just lint always noisy, requiring manual filtering to confirm only touched crates are clean
REST API for GitHub PR reviews does not expose .resolved state on threads — required a separate GraphQL query to confirm all 6 threads were resolved
open_items: moltis-h0c: Implement RecencyPreserving mode — head+tail verbatim preservation with tool-output pruning pre-pass and no LLM; estimated ~300-500 lines in compaction_run.rs

moltis-aff: Implement Structured mode — head+LLM-summary-of-middle+tail with Goal/Progress/Decisions template, iterative re-compaction state, tool-pair sanitization, and boundary alignment; estimated ~400-600 lines
moltis-8me: Auxiliary model subsystem — allow compaction to use a separate cheap provider/model (e.g. Gemini Flash) independent of the session model
Tool-pair repair (repairToolUseResultPairing equivalent) needed before RecencyPreserving/Structured land since those modes keep partial history where orphaned tool_result messages become possible
Identifier-preservation policy (strict prompt prefix) not yet ported from openclaw — should be added to the LlmReplace/Structured prompt as a low-effort quality improvement
Split→summarize→merge multi-stage compaction (openclaw pattern) deferred — only needed for sessions larger than the context window
Quality-audit retries (openclaw's 973-line safeguard subsystem) explicitly deferred until quality bar warrants it
PR #653 (deterministic compaction baseline) still needs to merge before the follow-up compaction mode PRs can land on main
